english: 1. the letter h — 2. numeral: 29.

1. W. a yawn, *ha yoṅ dug* (ha yong dug) I cannot help yawning. - 2. breath, ha 'debs-pa (ha 'debs pa) to breathe. - 3. the sound of laughter, *hab-gód čó-čè* (hab rgod co ce) W. to laugh out or aloud, cf. ha-há (ha ha).

1. gós-kyi hwa (gos kyi hwa) collar of a coat. — 2. shin, shin-bone Sch. — 3. *hǒ, hǒ* (ho ho) W. very well! — 4. col. nearly = *pha* (pha) II., yonder, farther off, *há-la gyug* (ha la rgyug) C. get you gone! be off! begone!; *há-čog* = *phá-rol* (ha phyogs = pha rol) the other side, yonder side, *há-gi* (ha gi) that (man) there, *há-gi-ru* (ha gi ru) there, there above, up there, there behind, thither, that way C., W.
W. to understand, *ha mi go-a* (ha mi go a) don't you understand it?

very, sometimes too much, too, mya-ṅán-las 'da-ba há-čaṅ yaṅ myur-čés-so (mya ngan las 'da ba ha cang yang myur ces so) his removal (prop. disappearing) from misery happens really too soon! Dzl.; há-čaṅ-nas (ha cang nas) id., *ha-čaṅ-né k'e-pa* (ha cang nas mkhas pa) C. very learned, possessed of extensive information.

wylie: ha no ho ne, han hon
english: very angry, much enraged Ld.
all of them, all together, in a body Sch.
to breathe out steam or vapour.
n. of a medicinal herb Lt.
woe (to you etc.)! W.
with *gyáb-čè* (rgyab ce), W. to play at dice.
W. impetuous, violent, rude, impudent.
with *čó-čè* (co ce) W. to deny, disown, disavow.
Ssk. parrot.
Ssk. vegetables, greens, pot-herbs Wdn.
Ssk. the turmeric plant, Curcuma S.g.
Ssk. sandal-wood.
Pur. centipede.
Ld. music (?).
Ssk. a medicine Med.

wylie: ha la, ha la ha la
english: Wdn., a certain poison, also poison in general, Ssk.
Cs.: a name of spyan-ras-gzigs (spyan ras gzigs), v. spyan (spyan).
about, near, nearly, pretty, tolerably, rather, de daṅ há-lam 'dra-ba (de dang ha lam 'dra ba) about or nearly like that Wdn.
Ar., *ha-lál čó-čè* (ha lal co ce), Urd. حلال کرنا to kill (an animal) in the manner prescribed by the Mahometan law W.
col., astonished, frightened, *ha-la-si* (ha las si) or *ha-la-se* (ha las se) id., *ha-lé čá-čè* (ha las cha ce) W., ha-lás-pa (ha las pa) B. to be astonished, frightened Mil., Pth.
flower, esp. a large beautiful garden-flower Glr., ha-ló rkyáṅ-pa (ha lo rkyang pa) a simple flower, ha-ló stoṅ-'dáb (ha lo stong 'dab) a double flower; *ha-ló-ka* (ha lo ka) mallow W. (?)
1. Cs.: (Chinese word) a Buddhist priest, doctor, scholar Glr. - 2. id., represented by a mask in religious plays, ha-šáṅ (ha shang) an old doctor with boys, his pupils.
a mineral medicine, used as a remedy for the stone; acc. to Wdn. = tód-le-kór (thod le kor), alabaster.
the natural sound of laughter Mil., ha-há rgód-pa (ha ha rgod pa) to laugh out, to set up a loud laugh;
